claims.statement: Local causal influence does not imply cross-domain certification authority.

failure_condition.description: A routing-only or persistent internal state creates or strengthens an EvidenceLink without a declared grounding rule and observation artifact.

claims.notes: ADOPTED records program adoption, not empirical confirmation. No authority-specific historical case is linked yet; the current implementation covers only part of the possible certification domains.

claims.statement: Within the versioned scenario-to-DynamicsEngine execution path, a processed temporal envelope preserves occurred_at <= available_at <= processed_at, processing time is monotonic, processed_at does not exceed the run's final_sim_time, and the engine path assigns the processing timestamp.

failure_condition.description: A delivery is processed with an inverted timestamp order, processed_at regresses or exceeds final_sim_time, or an input-supplied processed_at is accepted as the engine processing stamp.

claims.notes: This is a protocol and trace integrity result, not evidence for a human subjective-time law or a capability-secure Python object constructor. Direct record construction is an unsafe test surface outside the versioned scenario execution contract.

claims.statement: A transport redelivery with the same occurrence identity and canonical payload is idempotent across human-state updates and observation or evidence ledgers.

failure_condition.description: A second delivery of the same occurrence and canonical payload applies a second descriptive update, appends a second observation artifact, or strengthens evidence as an independent observation.

claims.statement: Reusing an occurrence identity with a different canonical payload is a hard identity collision and cannot be silently accepted as either a redelivery or a new occurrence.

failure_condition.description: Two different canonical payloads carrying the same occurrence_id are both accepted, one silently overwrites the other, or the second is treated as an ordinary duplicate.

claims.statement: Delayed availability or processing preserves the source occurrence time instead of rewriting a backlog item as a newly occurred event.

failure_condition.description: Processing a delayed delivery replaces its original occurred_at with available_at, processed_at, or the current sim_time.

claims.statement: An explicit current reexposure is distinct from transport redelivery: it may produce a new descriptive update while preserving a reference to the past source and cannot automatically strengthen the source EvidenceLink.

failure_condition.description: An explicitly identified current reexposure is collapsed into transport duplication solely because it references a past source, rewrites the past occurred_at, or creates independent source evidence without a new grounding observation.

claims.notes: The structural test demonstrates one legal descriptive update; it does not establish how human reexposure generally behaves.

claims.statement: The Q-v1 derived ledger contains exactly one qualification receipt per processed occurrence, its mental transitions are exactly the qualified receipt subset, and transport duplicates or redundant deliveries create neither an extra receipt nor a transition.

failure_condition.description: A processed occurrence has zero or multiple Q receipts, a qualified receipt lacks its mental transition, an unqualified receipt creates one, or a transport duplicate or redundant delivery adds either record.

claims.statement: Mental-transition qualification is an immutable post-run derived projection: changing Q-v1 threshold or field scope may change only the mental-transition ledger and cannot change the base HumanState trajectory, processing trace, evidence records, or action records.

failure_condition.description: Holding the scenario and non-Q engine configuration fixed while changing only Q policy changes a non-mental-transition state or ledger, or a derived report is fed back into the run that generated it.

claims.notes: Read-only means no feedback in the implemented run path; it does not prohibit a future separately specified experiment from consuming a prior report.

claims.statement: Q-v1 uses only current-checkpoint trace information, assigns transition_effective_at and qualified_at to processed_at, and later events cannot requalify an unchanged processing-trace prefix.

failure_condition.description: Appending later events changes prior receipts or transitions for an identical processing prefix, or a qualified transition is assigned to source occurrence or availability time rather than processed_at.

claims.statement: Canonical elapsed time, per-processed-occurrence qualification receipt, qualified mental transition, qualified count, and qualified density are distinct typed quantities or records and cannot be freely cast into one another.

failure_condition.description: A canonical schema aliases processed receipt, qualified transition, elapsed duration, qualified count, and density; uses transport deliveries as transitions; or permits an unrecorded cast between these quantities.

claims.notes: IMPLEMENTED is limited to Q-v1 simulation types and derived reports. It does not adopt HM-DYN-001 or identify a natural unit of consciousness.

claims.statement: Pre-transition DeformationDemand, typed CapacityProfile, their relative MorphicLoadProfile, achieved MorphicWorkReceipt, ResidualStrain, TraceCandidate, and horizon-qualified PersistentTrace are distinct quantities and records.

failure_condition.description: A canonical schema defines demand using post-update change, applies capacity both inside demand and again inside load, or records an immediate TraceCandidate as PersistentTrace without a retention horizon.

claims.notes: This is a proposed-model hygiene constraint, not support for the existence or predictive value of morphic load.

claims.statement: MorphicLoadProfile, a future PhenomenalStrainReadout, a phenomenal report, and external EvidenceLink are distinct records with no automatic cast between them.

failure_condition.description: A schema aliases load, phenomenal readout, report, and evidence or permits one of them to instantiate another without an explicit typed bridge and the required certification provenance.

claims.notes: The separation is adopted; the phenomenal bridge itself remains HOLD.

claims.statement: Source occurrence time, protocol availability time, engine processing time, and the engine simulation-time frontier are distinct temporal fields with different writers.

failure_condition.description: A canonical event or processing trace aliases occurred_at, available_at, processed_at, and sim_time into one untyped timestamp or gives the protocol input authority to pre-certify processed_at.

claims.notes: Implemented status is limited to timestamp schema, writer ownership, and trace preservation. FlowUpdate and EventJump are not part of this claim.

claims.statement: Occurrence identity, transport delivery identity, and an explicit current-reexposure reference are distinct provenance roles and cannot be freely substituted for one another.

failure_condition.description: A schema uses delivery_id as source occurrence identity, treats every new delivery as a new occurrence, or represents current reexposure only by duplicating the original occurrence.

claims.notes: The type split is a protocol provenance contract and makes no empirical claim about human event segmentation.

claims.statement: Preregistered qualified mental-transition density predicts held-out descriptive trajectories beyond canonical elapsed time, occurrence identity, and simpler event-count alternatives.

failure_condition.description: Under preregistered qualification and held-out comparisons, transition density adds no predictive or explanatory discrimination beyond canonical elapsed time, occurrence identity, and simpler event-count alternatives.

claims.notes: HM-DEFECT-EVENT-COUNT-SLOW-UPDATE-V01 motivates separating time from event count but does not support this predictive human hypothesis.

claims.statement: At matched canonical time and qualified transition count, preregistered DeformationDemand and CapacityProfile relations predict held-out recovery, residual strain, or horizon-qualified trace retention better than a count-only model after complexity penalties.

failure_condition.description: Across preregistered count-load dissociation and capacity-relative tests, typed demand and capacity add no held-out discrimination over a simpler transition-count model after complexity penalties.

claims.statement: A morphic-load measurement must define units, normalization, and missingness rules; estimate typed DeformationDemand from pre-transition shape and incoming constraint; separately derive MorphicLoadProfile from demand and pre-transition CapacityProfile; record achieved work and residual strain; and withhold PersistentTrace status until a preregistered retention horizon.

failure_condition.description: Demand cannot be estimated before the modeled update, demand and capacity contributions to load are not separately identifiable, achieved work cannot be distinguished from residual strain, or trace retention cannot be calibrated at the preregistered horizon.

claims.statement: Q-v1 is a simulation measurement model that evaluates one processed-occurrence checkpoint at a time over versioned literal persistent descriptive fields, records typed before/after deltas, qualifies a receipt when at least one absolute delta reaches 0.01 normalized_simulation_unit by default, and reports qualified count and density as distinct typed quantities.

failure_condition.description: The declared policy cannot reproduce its typed deltas or qualification from the current checkpoint trace, threshold or field-scope changes are not identified by the policy digest, undeclared fields enter the scope, or count, duration, and density cannot be recovered as distinct report quantities.

claims.notes: Calibration here means internal reproducibility and identifiability in normalized simulation units. No human threshold, natural mental-time unit, or predictive advantage has been calibrated. The default scope includes access fields still influenced by legacy_v01_access_pressure_bridge; excluding the queue object does not remove that indirect confound. Per-processed-occurrence Q-v1 also does not guarantee stability under unresolved same-time ordering or alternative semantic bundling. Queue/access decoupling or scope ablation and a separate serialization failure probe are required before HM-DYN-001 evaluation.

claims.statement: The slime-shape image is an explanatory metaphor for current form, admissible deformation range, incoming constraint, required reformation, and residual strain.

failure_condition.description: The slime metaphor is cited as empirical or physical evidence, used to supply an unmeasured unit, or treated as proof of the morphic-load hypothesis.
